channel.title: New Zealand Programming Contest
channel.description: Recent content on New Zealand Programming Contest

item.title: Contest Documentation
item.description:
Everything you need to know about entering and competing in the New Zealand Programming Contest. Start with the rules, then read the registration guide for your category.

§

Contest rules

Categories and grades, scoring, allowed languages, and conduct on contest day.

Read the rules → 🏫

Guide – School teams

Eligibility and registration for secondary-school and home-school pupils.

School guide → 🎓

Guide – Tertiary teams

Junior, Intermediate and Open tertiary categories explained.

Tertiary guide → 👤

Guide – Open category

How anyone outside school and tertiary study can enter and compete.

item.title: Contest Registration
item.description:
Registration is handled per site

Whether pre-registration is needed, how it's done, and the entry fee all depend on your site – contact your site coordinator (below) for details. It's often not required, and mainly helps your site plan catering and seating.

Most sites reserve places through TeamUp with the code NZPC2026; your coordinator will confirm whether this applies.

Site University / Host Contact Person More Information
Auckland University of Auckland Marc Vinyals Site Page
Hamilton University of Waikato Tom Levy Site Page
Wellington Wellington Institute of Technology Chalinor Baliuag Site Page
Christchurch University of Canterbury Ke He Site Page
Dunedin University of Otago Tony Savarimuthu/Zhiyi Huang Site Page
Invercargill James Hargest College Sarah Greeff Site Page

Before you register

Read the contest documentation – the rules and the registration guide for your category – before contest day.

item.title: Contest Rules
item.description:
Document last updated on Thursday, 31 July 2025.

Contest categories and grades

This contest has five categories of entry. Teams compete only against teams from the same category – there are thus five winners.

  • School
  • Tertiary – Junior
  • Tertiary – Intermediate
  • Tertiary – Open
  • Open

These categories are described in the registration guides.

Registration

Teams register through DOMjudge at the start of the contest. Reserving a place and the entry fee are handled per site – usually through TeamUp – so team managers should check the Registration page and contact their site coordinator for the details.

item.title: Registration Guide – Open Category Teams
item.description:
Eligibility requirements

This category is open to anyone living in New Zealand at the time of the contest who has had no part in preparing any of the problem set.

Registration

To register, a team requires a team manager who will undertake to pay the entry fee for the team (typically $120, but the amount is set per site – confirm with your site coordinator). Note that there is no fee if the contest is on-line. The team manager may be one of the contestants.

item.title: Registration Guide – School Teams
item.description:
Eligibility requirements

To register as a contestant for a school team, you must be a pupil at a secondary school in New Zealand, which includes a home school.

There are two types of school team.

  1. A team where all contestants are from the same school. This type of team may win both the individual top award and the top school award.
  2. A team where the contestants are from two or three different schools. This type of team may win the individual top award, but not the top school award.

Registration

To register, a team requires an adult team manager who is able to confirm that the contestants meet the eligibility requirements, and will undertake to pay the entry fee for the team (typically $30, but the amount is set per site – confirm with your site coordinator). Note that there is no fee if the contest is on-line. A teacher or a parent would normally fill this role.

item.title: Registration Guide – Tertiary Teams
item.description:
Eligibility requirements

To register as a contestant for a tertiary team, you must be a student at a tertiary institution in New Zealand, with at least a 50% timetable during the semester of the contest.

There are three categories for tertiary teams.

  • Junior. Normally students in their first year of programming, who have not taken any second year papers. They would not be particularly fluent yet.
  • Intermediate. Normally students in their second year of programming, who have not taken any third year papers. They would be reasonably fluent in 1 language.
  • Open. Tertiary students who do not meet the requirements of the other categories.

A team must compete in the category of the highest rated student, eg a team with two first year students and one second year student must compete as Intermediate.

item.title: Contest categories
item.description:
Notes

  1. If team members are not in the same category, then the team must enter in the highest category of the members.
  2. The fees below are typical amounts. Entry fees are set and collected per site to cover their costs (eg pizzas), and some sites do not charge at all – contact your site coordinator to confirm.
  3. Entry fees do NOT apply for an on-line contest.
Category Description Typical Fee
School Team members must all attend a secondary school in New Zealand. This includes a home school. $30
Tertiary Junior Usually first year university or polytechnic students. Have not started any second year papers. $75
Tertiary Intermediate Usually second year university or polytechnic students. Have not started any third year papers. $75
Tertiary Open University or polytechnic students who do not qualify for the previous 2 categories. $75
Open Anybody not eligible for categories 1 to 4. Usually professional programmers. $120

item.title: Whitireia and WelTec NZPC Site Page
item.description:
The 2026 NZ Programming Contest will be held later in the year, probably in August.

Local site information (at Wellington Institute of Technology)

Venue

Room T605/T606, T-block, WelTec, Petone Campus (21 Kensington Ave., Petone).

Languages

  • GNU C/C++ Version 10.2 (Linux)
  • Java OpenJDK V11.0.14

Other languages can possibly be supplied if requested sufficiently in advance.

OS / Hardware / etc

We will use the DOMjudge automated judge system for running the contest.
